summary:
Proparacaine has a brief duration of effect similar to related local anesthetics; thus, its action lasts only a few minutes before waning. Given this rapid decline, it does not maintain potency beyond several minutes post-administration. \boxed{B}
The summary captures the essence by highlighting the brevity of proparacaine’s efficacy based on comparisons with known properties of analogous drugs while adhering strictly to concise presentation as requested.
